## 1. Does your platform encrypt data at rest, and if so, what encryption standard do you use?

Based on [kb-001]: Does your platform support encryption of data at rest? Yes. All customer data is encrypted at rest using AES-256. Encryption keys are managed via our dedicated key management service and rotated quarterly. This applies to all persistent storage layers including databases and object storage. Based on [kb-002]: What encryption is used for data in transit? All data in transit is protected using TLS 1.2 or higher. We enforce strict TLS on all API endpoints and internal service-to-service communication. Older protocol versions (TLS 1.0, 1.1) are disabled. Based on [kb-012]: What is your SLA for uptime? Our standard SLA guarantees 99.9% monthly uptime for production environments. Enterprise plans include a 99.95% SLA. We publish a real-time status page and provide root-cause analyses for incidents exceeding 15 minutes of downtime. Based on [kb-006]: Does your platform support single sign-on (SSO)? Yes. We support SAML 2.0 and OIDC-based SSO integration with all major identity providers including Okta, Azure AD, and Google Workspace. SSO configuration is available on enterprise plans and can be set up by your IT administrator using our integration guide. Based on [kb-004]: What is your data retention policy? Customer data is retained for the duration of the subscription plus 90 days after contract termination. Upon customer request or at end of retention period, data is securely deleted using NIST 800-88 guidelines. Backup media is overwritten within 30 days.
